key_insight: A transfer-learned physics-informed neural network can estimate lithium-ion battery states accurately by reusing electrochemical knowledge across different operating conditions and chemistries.
engineering_relevance: This approach can cut training cost and improve scalability for battery state estimation while preserving the physics needed for reliable digital-twin and BMS applications.
method: Pretrain a PINN on the single particle model with electrolyte (SPMe) to learn general electrochemical behavior.
Transfer the learned weights to a target battery, freeze selected layers, and fine-tune the remaining parameters.
Estimate key electrochemical states while enforcing conservation laws and voltage consistency in the loss function.
evidence_signal: Validation with PyBaMM shows accurate voltage prediction, suggesting the transfer-learning PINN maintains electrochemical consistency while improving training efficiency.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the pretrained source battery matches the target battery, and layer-freezing choices can affect convergence and accuracy.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Network with Transfer Learning for State Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries using the Single Particle Model with Electrolyte
